title: Cache LLM élargi scans œuvres répétitifs (lift image bypass via SHA-256 visual signature)

C3 only modifies the bypass guard, NOT the hit-return path, so AC11 is preserved by construction. NOT a blocker.

correctness.rationale: All 26 EARS (R1-R26) and 11 testable ACs (AC1-AC8 + AC12-AC14) satisfied by the unit tests + production diff. Key correctness checks: (a) Hash post-EXIF-strip — verified at image-processing.service.ts:134 (upload branch) and :165 (legacy branch) both call `hashImageBuffer(stripped.buffer)` where `stripped.buffer` is the OUTPUT of `stripExif()` (line 192 returns `cleaned.buffer`); the test R5 (c3-llm-cache.test.ts:366-390) explicitly asserts hash matches stripped output, NOT input. (b) Url-source skip — verified at image-processing.service.ts:86-91 (returns `{imageRef, orchestratorImage}` WITHOUT imageContentHash key) — test R2/AC2 locks `undefined`. (c) Conditional spread for AC6 byte-identity — verified at llm-cache.service.ts:152-154 (`if (input.imageContentHash !== undefined) canonical.imageContentHash = input.imageContentHash;`) — test R8 (c3-llm-cache.test.ts:149-172) locks both omit-path AND explicit `undefined` byte-identity. (d) Bypass narrowing — verified at chat-message.service.ts:330-336 (lookup) and :357-362 (store) — `hasImage && !hasVisualSignature` is the ONLY new condition; symmetric in lookup/store (R13). (e) Plumbing — verified at prepare-message.pipeline.ts:215-220 (processInputImage returns imageContentHash) → :281 (PrepareReady.imageContentHash threaded into ctx.prep) → chat-message.service.ts:336 + :360 + :488 (buildLlmCacheInput propagates). (f) Hash compute — `hashImageBuffer` (image-processing.service.ts:237-239) uses `createHash('sha256').update(buffer).digest('hex').slice(0, 32)` matching the canonical hash length convention.
correctness.evidence: image-processing.service.ts:108-135 (upload + post-strip hash) ; image-processing.service.ts:143-165 (legacy + post-strip hash) ; image-processing.service.ts:86-91 (url branch — NO hash field) ; image-processing.service.ts:178-194 (stripExif now returns `buffer`) ; image-processing.service.ts:237-239 (hashImageBuffer helper) ; llm-cache.service.ts:140-160 (conditional spread sha256OfCanonicalInput) ; llm-cache.types.ts:21-31 (imageContentHash JSDoc honest UFR-013) ; prepare-message.pipeline.ts:41-46 + :196-220 + :281 (plumbing) ; chat-message.service.ts:330-336 / :357-362 / :485-489 (bypass + buildLlmCacheInput). 21/21 c3-llm-cache tests green.
minorNotes: N1 — AC9/AC10/AC11 are not asserted via unit tests in c3-llm-cache.test.ts (the spec explicitly defers them to integration-flavored tests in chat-message-service.test.ts, see spec line 394-402). The contract is locked at the service-level (R12 invariant test at c3-llm-cache.test.ts:404-422) and structurally enforceable by inspection of the diff. Acceptable for ship — but a follow-up green-phase test landing AC9-AC11 explicitly in chat-message-service.test.ts is recommended (not a blocker).

N2 — The `hashString16(prep.userMemoryBlock)` produces a 16-char hex while the new `imageContentHash` is 32 chars. This intentional asymmetry (16 chars for prefs, 32 for image) is honest per spec §2.1 collision math, but the comments could note the asymmetry explicitly. Sub-cosmetic.

N3 — `hashImageBuffer` is a private module-level function (image-processing.service.ts:237-239) — not exported. If a future test wants to hash a fixture buffer for direct comparison, it reconstructs the formula. Test files already do this (c3-llm-cache.test.ts:89-90 `expectedHashOf`). Acceptable.

scope-fidelity.rationale: ZERO modification outside the 5 declared production files (image-processing.service.ts + llm-cache.types.ts + llm-cache.service.ts + chat-message.service.ts + prepare-message.pipeline.ts). ZERO FE touch (museum-frontend/ untouched in diff). ZERO ADR-036 strategy change : single-layer L1 preserved (no L2 decorator), key prefix `llm:v1:` preserved (line 14-15 unchanged), TTL constants preserved (line 18-20 unchanged — no new constant for image-cache path per R18), kill-switch preserved (chat-message.service.ts:330 `if (!llmCache) return null;` short-circuits before any image-aware logic). ZERO new file (5 files modified, 1 new test file under tests/unit/chat/c3-llm-cache.test.ts). ZERO new dependency (`crypto` is Node built-in). The bypass narrowing is the only behavioral change : image presence alone is no longer a bypass condition (R11) — but the URL-source / processing-failed paths preserve the original bypass (R12) via `hasImage && !hasVisualSignature`. Backward compat AC6/R8 is the load-bearing invariant — locked by the conditional spread + the test at c3-llm-cache.test.ts:149-172. Spec deferrals respected: no semantic similarity (Q2), no per-image invalidation (Q3, R20), no TTL tune (R18 — ADR-036 R11 bake protocol gated).
scope-fidelity.evidence: `git diff --stat 38d0aa23b -- museum-backend museum-frontend` (working tree) shows 5 BE files modified + 1 new BE test file ; museum-frontend = 0 files. ADR-036 alignment table in spec C3.md:276-285 verified line-by-line against the diff.

kiss-dry-hexagonal.rationale: Hash compute logic centralized in `hashImageBuffer` module-level helper (image-processing.service.ts:237-239) — single SOT for the 32-char prefix convention, no duplication across upload/legacy branches (both call the helper). `stripExif` private signature extended to return `buffer` alongside base64/mimeType/sizeBytes — minimal allocation (the buffer is already constructed for the magic-byte assertion or imageProcessor input). Conditional spread in `sha256OfCanonicalInput` (llm-cache.service.ts:152-154) — pragmatic, preserves AC6 byte-identity without introducing a separate canonical builder. Plumbing through PrepareReady is one optional field (no new envelope). Bypass logic remains a 2-line guard in chat-message.service.ts, symmetric across lookup/store (R13). Hexagonal: zero crossing of the chat module boundary, zero adapter-level injection of cache concerns. The ADR-036 single-layer invariant holds. Minor DRY note: the `hasImage && !hasVisualSignature` check is duplicated in tryLlmCacheLookup (line 333-336) and tryLlmCacheStore (line 360-362) — by spec R13 (symmetry), but a tiny shared predicate `shouldBypassImageCache(ctx)` could DRY it. Not a blocker — the duplication is 3 lines × 2 sites, intentional for grep-ability.
kiss-dry-hexagonal.evidence: image-processing.service.ts:237-239 (single hash helper) ; image-processing.service.ts:178-194 (stripExif buffer reuse, no extra allocation) ; llm-cache.service.ts:140-160 (conditional spread one-liner) ; chat-message.service.ts:330-336 + :357-362 (symmetric 2-line guard).
minorNotes: N4 — `hasImage && !hasVisualSignature` duplicated lookup vs store (DRY). Acceptable: 3 lines × 2 sites, intentional symmetry for grep-ability (R13). Optional refactor to `shouldBypassImageCache(ctx)` private method — not required.

N5 — The 32-char hex slice convention is hard-coded twice: in `hashImageBuffer` (image-processing.service.ts:238) and in `sha256OfCanonicalInput` (llm-cache.service.ts:160). Both honored independently — if one ever changes, the other does NOT need to (they hash different inputs into different fields). Honest: NOT a coupling, just a stylistic convention. No DRY violation.

a11y-design-system.rationale: BE-only feature. No UI surface. No FE diff. axis weight redistributed across the 4 remaining axes (see weightedMeanCalc).
a11y-design-system.evidence: museum-frontend diff = 0 ; npm test PASS 2410/2410 unchanged.

security-honesty.rationale: Privacy: SHA-256 of post-strip buffer is one-way — no PII leak. R21 honored — logs include `hasImage: boolean` (chat-message.service.ts:347 + :371) but NEVER the raw hash or buffer bytes; the canonical hash itself is embedded in the cache KEY only (Redis backend), not in any Pino log line. Verified by grep: no `imageContentHash` reference in any logger.info/warn call. Tenant isolation: museumId remains in the key prefix BEFORE the canonical hash (llm-cache.service.ts:134) — cross-museum sharing structurally impossible (R19, R22). User isolation: userId segment also preserved BEFORE the hash — two users scanning the same artwork still produce different keys (R22). Naming honesty (UFR-013): field is `imageContentHash` everywhere (types, service, helper, JSDoc) — explicit comment at llm-cache.types.ts:23-25 + image-processing.service.ts:30-39 calls out `NOT a SigLIP / vector embedding hash`, content-bytes only. The tracking line 72 used `artworkSigLIPHash` historically; spec Q4 explicitly chose `imageContentHash` for honesty. Verified: zero occurrence of `siglip` or `SigLIP` in the C3 production diff (case-insensitive grep). Failure-mode honesty: `hashImageBuffer` runs on a Buffer that already exists in memory (post-strip), in-process, synchronous — cannot fail; if `createHash` ever threw, the existing try/catch in `tryLlmCacheLookup`/`tryLlmCacheStore` catches and continues (fail-open per ADR-036). Kill-switch: `LLM_CACHE_ENABLED=false` continues to short-circuit at chat-message.service.ts:330,357 (R14). No new storage of raw bytes (R23) — the hash is computed inline and the buffer is already on the existing `imageStorage.save()` path.
security-honesty.evidence: image-processing.service.ts:30-39 (UFR-013 honest JSDoc — NOT SigLIP) ; llm-cache.types.ts:23-25 (same honest comment) ; chat-message.service.ts:347 + :371 (hasImage in log fields — Boolean, not hex) ; llm-cache.service.ts:130-135 (key shape museumId BEFORE hash — R19/R22 tenant isolation) ; image-processing.service.ts:237-239 (hashImageBuffer in-process, infallible).
minorNotes: N6 — `userId='anon'` collapses into a shared-cache lane for anonymous in-museum scans (same artwork + same locale + no prefs ≈ same answer). R22 documents this as UFR-013-acceptable: no user-specific input is in the canonical, no PII can leak. Honest tradeoff.
N7 — The 32-char hex prefix is 128 bits of entropy = collision probability ≈ 10⁻²⁰ over 10⁹ entries (spec §2.1 + R4 comment). Honest math, validated by R4 1-byte-diff test.

N8 — `LLM_CACHE_ENABLED=false` env-flag short-circuit is `if (!llmCache) return null;` (chat-message.service.ts:330, 357). The wiring point that translates env → `this.llmCache = undefined` lives in the module composition root (chat module index/factory) — not in C3 scope, but verified externally that R14 holds (kill-switch precedes ALL C3 logic).

niceToHave.title: AC9/AC10/AC11 unit-level integration coverage in chat-message-service.test.ts
niceToHave.evidence: Spec C3.md:394-402 explicitly defers the end-to-end happy-path (image present + hash plumbed → lookup happens → hit returns cached output) to integration tests at chat-message.service level. The c3-llm-cache.test.ts:404-422 locks the contract invariant at service level (key DOES embed imageContentHash). The plumbing is structurally verified by inspection of the diff.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Acceptable for ship: the unit-level invariant + the structural inspection of buildLlmCacheInput + the bypass narrowing diff cover the AC9-AC11 surface area. A green-phase follow-up test landing the full chat-message.service postMessage happy-path would strengthen the safety net.
niceToHave.remediation: Optional: add `it('AC9/R11 — calls llmCache.lookup with imageContentHash when image is plumbed and text non-empty', ...)` in chat-message-service.test.ts. Estimated +20 LOC of test code. Not required for done.

niceToHave.title: shouldBypassImageCache helper (DRY across lookup/store)
niceToHave.evidence: The 2-line guard `const hasImage = …; const hasVisualSignature = …; if (hasImage && !hasVisualSignature) return null;` is duplicated symmetrically in tryLlmCacheLookup and tryLlmCacheStore per R13 (symmetry invariant).
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Intentional duplication for grep-ability and the R13 symmetry locked-by-construction. Refactoring to a shared predicate is a stylistic preference. Mirror pattern with other guard pairs in the file (e.g., the `if (!llmCache)` short-circuit appears in both methods).
niceToHave.remediation: Optional: extract `private shouldBypassImageCache(ctx: LlmCacheCtx): boolean`. Sub-cosmetic.

niceToHave.title: `hashString16(userMemoryBlock)` produces 16 hex chars vs `imageContentHash` 32 hex chars — asymmetry not commented
niceToHave.evidence: The prefs hash is 64 bits (16 hex), the image hash is 128 bits (32 hex). Both feed `sha256OfCanonicalInput` which then produces a 32-hex output anyway, so the asymmetry in INPUT entropy is functionally invisible at the final cache key. Spec §2.1 collision math justifies 32-char for image (10⁹ entries → 10⁻²⁰); prefs hash 16-char is pre-C3 inherited convention (4 × 10⁹ collisions at 10⁻⁹ probability — likely intentional for user-scoped scope).
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Pre-C3 convention preserved (R4-R5 of ADR-036). Honest but un-commented. A one-line comment near `hashString16` or `hashImageBuffer` would clarify for future maintainers.
niceToHave.remediation: Optional: add JSDoc note `/** 32 hex (128 bits) — image collision resistance vs prefs hashString16. */` near `hashImageBuffer`. Sub-cosmetic.

niceToHave.title: tracking.md line 72 still references `artworkSigLIPHash` historical naming
niceToHave.evidence: Spec Q4 (C3.md:418-423) explicitly chose `imageContentHash` for UFR-013 honesty. The tracking note may still say `artworkSigLIPHash` from the initial bootstrap. Update at done-flip per Q4 closing line.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Not a code issue. Tracking-doc consistency only.
niceToHave.remediation: Update tracking.md line 72 at the same commit that flips C3 status to `done`. Trivial.

T1-no-semantic-matching-v1.evidence: Spec Q2 + §5 out-of-scope explicit: V1 ships content-hash exact-match only (R8 reuse of ADR-036 'exact-match only' lock). Two visually-similar but byte-different photos of the same artwork produce different hashes → different entries. Hit rate floor: only byte-identical re-submissions (re-tap send on same captured frame, retry after Wi-Fi flake within the 24h museum-mode TTL window). Bake measurement post-deploy will reveal whether to escalate to SigLIP-embedding-based key derivation (separate ADR proposal). Honest deferral.

T2-no-per-image-invalidation.evidence: R20 + Q3: invalidateMuseum(museumId) drops ALL museum-scoped entries (existing path covers C3 entries because museumId is in key prefix BEFORE sha256). Per-image invalidation deferred V1.1 if museum-update frequency rises. Honest math: pre-launch museum CRUD is rare.

T3-no-ttl-tune-pre-bake.evidence: R18 + ADR-036 R11 bake protocol: ≥7 consecutive days of Grafana `llm_cache_hits_total{context_class}` measurement required BEFORE any TTL tune PR. C3 ships with the existing 24h museum-mode (most common image-scan case) / 1h personalized / 7d generic. ADR amendment deferred per spec Q1 — optional doc-only amendment may land alongside C3 if dispatcher chooses.

T4-post-strip-not-pre-strip-hash.evidence: R5 + Q5 locked: hash is computed on the post-EXIF-strip buffer so two uploads of the same photo with different EXIF (timezone, camera serial) produce the SAME hash and hit the same cache. EXIF strip is deterministic (sharp `rotate().toBuffer()` per spec Q6). Test R5 (c3-llm-cache.test.ts:366-390) locks the post-strip invariant via a mocked stripExif that returns a deterministic shortened buffer.

T5-32-char-hex-vs-full-sha256.evidence: Spec §2.1 + R4 collision math: 32 hex = 128 bits ≈ 10⁻²⁰ collision probability over 10⁹ entries. Aligned with the existing `sha256OfCanonicalInput` 32-char convention. Honest tradeoff vs. full 64-hex (256 bits) — the marginal collision resistance gain is irrelevant at Musaium scale.

T6-naming-imagecontenthash-not-siglip.evidence: UFR-013 honesty per Q4: the field is a SHA-256 of bytes, NOT a SigLIP embedding hash. Code, types, JSDoc, test names, and spec all use `imageContentHash`. Grep `siglip|SigLIP` over the C3 production diff returns 0 hits. Tracking line 72 to be updated at done-flip.

T7-bypass-symmetry-lookup-store.evidence: R13: lookup bypass and store bypass apply IDENTICAL conditions. Verified by inspection of chat-message.service.ts:330-336 vs :357-362 — both use `if (!llmCache) return null; const hasImage = …; const hasVisualSignature = …; if (hasImage && !hasVisualSignature) return null/return;`. Invariant: miss→store→hit replay preserved.

adr036Alignment.failOpen: PRESERVED — try/catch in lookup/store remains untouched ; createHash on Buffer is infallible in practice
adr036Alignment.exactMatchOnly: PRESERVED — content-hash, NOT embedding-hash ; semantic similarity remains G-Phase-2 deferred
adr036Alignment.ttlTuneProtocol: APPLIES — no TTL change in C3 ; future tune gated by ADR-036 R11 ≥7-day bake + amendment ; spec Q1 leaves ADR documentation-amendment as dispatcher choice

recommandation.rationale: weightedMean 95 ≥ 85 threshold. Zero blocker, zero important finding. 4 nice-to-haves: N1 (AC9-AC11 integration tests — spec-deferred, structurally locked), N2 (DRY bypass predicate — stylistic), N3 (hash asymmetry comment — sub-cosmetic), N4 (tracking.md naming consistency — to update at done-flip). 26/26 EARS + 11/14 ACs unit-covered + 3 ACs structurally verified. Lint + 21/21 C3 tests + chat scope 167/168 (env redis failure pre-existing) + FE 2410/2410 unchanged. 5/5 Musaium gates pass. ADR-036 alignment 8/8 invariants preserved. Honesty score 95 — UFR-013 naming + tenant isolation + no PII in logs + honest failure-mode documentation. C3 can flip `red → done` in tracking.md.
recommandation.merge-readiness: Ready. BE-only delta on 5 files (+ 1 new test file). Zero new dep. Zero FE touch. Single-layer ADR-036 invariant preserved. Backward-compat AC6/R8 byte-identity locked (legacy text-only cache entries remain valid post-deploy — no migration step). Bake observation per ADR-036 R11 begins at deploy ; TTL tune (if any) lands as separate PR with ADR amendment per protocol.
